task_id.description: Required. If the task is new, provide a unique ID. If the task already exists and needs to be updated, reuse the same ID.

depends_on.description: List of task IDs that must be completed before this task can begin. These may reference existing or newly defined tasks.

validated_requirements.description: List of requirement IDs this task satisfies. Must be included if the task fulfills any product requirement’s acceptance criteria.

completion_criteria.description: List of natural language statements describing when the task can be considered successfully complete.

role_assignee.description: Which agent or actor is responsible for executing this task.

design_handoff.description: Structured output from a DESIGN task, used for handoff to engineering tasks. May include component IDs, layout guidance, and design tokens.

phase.description: Determines whether the task represents a build-time activity or a runtime execution. Required for ENGINEERING tasks.

execution_mode.description: Optional. One of 'HOST' or 'CONTAINER'. Defaults to 'CONTAINER'. Use 'HOST' for tasks that create or build Docker containers or environments. Use 'CONTAINER' for all workload execution tasks.

requires_test.description: Indicates whether this task must be accompanied by an automated test. Defaults to true for application logic tasks. Set to false for infra/setup tasks (e.g., Dockerfile generation or container push).

task_type.description: Optional semantic subtype for EXECUTE-phase tasks to support tracking, filtering, and specialized handling. Defaults to RUN if omitted.

execution_schedule.description: Optional. Specifies how often this task should run. Defaults to 'ONCE'. Use 'DAEMON' for background tasks and 'HOURLY'/'DAILY'/'WEEKLY' for recurring execution.

execution_start_time.description: Optional ISO 8601 datetime string specifying when the task should first run. Used to anchor recurring schedules.
